req.title: Webhook delivery
req.statement: Outbound webhooks shall retry on 5xx with exponential backoff up to 5 times; permanent failures shall be DLQ'd.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates outbound webhook delivery with exponential-backoff retry (up to 5 attempts) on 5xx responses and DLQ placement on permanent failure. Four tests cover: (1) EP positive – successful delivery on first attempt (2xx); (2) NEG negative – all 5 retries exhaust on persistent 5xx leading to DLQ; (3) BVA boundary – exactly 5th retry succeeds (delivery on the maximum allowed attempt); (4) STC positive – webhook transitions through PENDING → RETRYING (×4) → DLQ when every attempt returns 5xx, verifying state machine correctness. The STC test is classified as positive because it validates the correct/expected state-transition behaviour of the system (the system is working as designed when it DLQs after exhausted retries); the negative failure scenario is already covered by the NEG test.

tests.name: test_webhook_delivered_on_first_attempt
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
Target URL returns HTTP 200 on first call
A triggering event is ready to fire
actions: Register a mock HTTP server that always responds 200
Configure webhook pointing to the mock server via <fixture: webhook_config>
Fire the triggering event via <helper_fn: fire_event>
Wait for delivery confirmation via <helper_fn: wait_for_delivery>
tests.executable:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch

def test_webhook_delivered_on_first_attempt(webhook_config, mock_target_server, fire_event, wait_for_delivery, dlq_client):
    mock_target_server.set_responses([200])
    hook_id = webhook_config.create(url=mock_target_server.url, event="user.created")

    fire_event("user.created", payload={"user_id": "u-001"})
    result = wait_for_delivery(hook_id, timeout=10)

    assert result.status == "DELIVERED"
    assert mock_target_server.call_count == 1
    assert dlq_client.exists(hook_id) is False

tests.name: test_webhook_dlq_after_all_retries_exhausted
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
A triggering event is ready to fire
actions: Register a mock HTTP server that always responds 500
Configure webhook pointing to the mock server
Wait until the system marks the delivery as failed
Inspect DLQ for the failed delivery entry
expected: Webhook endpoint is called exactly 6 times (1 initial + 5 retries)
A DLQ entry exists for the hook delivery
trace: R-WEB-110: permanent failures shall be DLQ'd
R-WEB-110: retry on 5xx up to 5 times
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_webhook_dlq_after_all_retries_exhausted(webhook_config, mock_target_server, fire_event, wait_for_delivery, dlq_client):
    mock_target_server.set_responses([500] * 6)
    hook_id = webhook_config.create(url=mock_target_server.url, event="user.created")

    fire_event("user.created", payload={"user_id": "u-002"})
    result = wait_for_delivery(hook_id, timeout=60)

    assert result.status == "FAILED"
    assert mock_target_server.call_count == 6, (
        f"Expected 6 attempts, got {mock_target_server.call_count}"
    )
    dlq_entry = dlq_client.get(hook_id)
    assert dlq_entry is not None
    assert dlq_entry["hook_id"] == hook_id

tests.name: test_webhook_delivered_on_exactly_fifth_retry
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
Target URL returns 500 for the first 5 calls then 200 on the 6th
A triggering event is ready to fire
actions: Register a mock HTTP server returning [500,500,500,500,500,200]
Configure webhook pointing to the mock server
expected: Webhook endpoint is called exactly 6 times
Delivery status is DELIVERED (success on the 5th retry, i.e. 6th call)
trace: R-WEB-110: retry up to 5 times – boundary at retry #5
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_webhook_delivered_on_exactly_fifth_retry(webhook_config, mock_target_server, fire_event, wait_for_delivery, dlq_client):
    responses = [500, 500, 500, 500, 500, 200]
    mock_target_server.set_responses(responses)
    hook_id = webhook_config.create(url=mock_target_server.url, event="user.updated")

    fire_event("user.updated", payload={"user_id": "u-003"})
    result = wait_for_delivery(hook_id, timeout=60)

    assert result.status == "DELIVERED"
    assert mock_target_server.call_count == 6, (
        f"Expected 6 attempts (1+5 retries), got {mock_target_server.call_count}"
    )
    assert dlq_client.exists(hook_id) is False

tests.name: test_webhook_state_transitions_pending_retrying_dlq
preconditions: Webhook endpoint is configured for hr-app under /hooks/*
Delivery state is observable via status API
actions: Register a mock HTTP server always returning 503
Poll delivery status after each expected retry interval
Wait until final state is reached
expected: Status transitions to RETRYING at least once before final state
DLQ entry is present after final failure
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time

def test_webhook_state_transitions_pending_retrying_dlq(webhook_config, mock_target_server, fire_event, get_delivery_status, dlq_client):
    mock_target_server.set_responses([503] * 6)
    hook_id = webhook_config.create(url=mock_target_server.url, event="user.deleted")

    fire_event("user.deleted", payload={"user_id": "u-004"})

    observed_states = []
    for _ in range(30):
        status = get_delivery_status(hook_id)
        if status not in observed_states:
            observed_states.append(status)
        if status in ("FAILED", "DELIVERED"):
            break
        time.sleep(2)

    assert "PENDING" in observed_states, "PENDING state never observed"
    assert "RETRYING" in observed_states, "RETRYING state never observed"
    assert observed_states[-1] == "FAILED"
    assert dlq_client.exists(hook_id) is True
